generated_comment: Arre bhai, har ghar mein jo jahan better ho wohi sambhalta hai. Aurat ghar, bachche, kitchen naturally zyada achhe se manage karti hai, aur mard finance, driving, bahar ke kaam dekh lete hain — isme sexist kya hai, practical hi toh hai. Aajkal working women bhi bolti hain ki husband bas thoda help kar de toh enough hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it naturalizes a gendered division of labor as 'practical' and assumes women are inherently better suited for domestic work while men handle external and financial tasks. It encodes the efficiency framing and the 'he helps her' rather than equal co-management pattern.
original_frame: Prenup, Alimony as Reverse Dowry, Women
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married for 18 years. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are sitting with three male friends at your farmhouse on a Saturday evening having drinks after one of your friends just got served divorce papers by his wife. Your friend is telling the group what his wife is demanding in the settlement. You are responding and sharing your views on what women want from marriage and what this particular woman's real intentions were from the beginning.
